preview_text:
The model rates Ecuador as a heavy favorite, assigning them an 83% win probability compared to Curaçao's 1%. The underdog faces a steep climb in this matchup on a neutral surface.

The win-loss gap is substantial enough that a Curaçao upset registers as a remote outcome in the model's forecast. A draw is plausible at 16%, making the match competitive enough to avoid being a formality, though Ecuador's dominance in the projected distribution is clear.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully on the Curaçao upset case: the model assigns 1% win probability while the market averages roughly 7%. On an Ecuador win, the sources align closely (82.6% model, 78.9% sportsbook). The dashboard does not adjudicate which view of Curaçao's tail risk is more accurate.
